qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default create/update/replace allow out-of-root archive reads via ../ paths
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is an extraction-time hardlink path traversal in `tar.x()`/`Unpack` caused by checking `..` before `stripAbsolutePath()` on drive-relative link targets. This finding is an archive-creation issue in `create`/`update`/`replace`, `Pack.add()`, and `WriteEntry` that reads `../` source paths outside `cwd`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Selective list/extract file filters crash on deeply nested archive entry paths
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory patch changes `src/unpack.ts` link-target sanitization for drive-relative hardlinks and symlinks. This finding is a separate denial-of-service bug in `filesFilter().mapHas()` recursion during selective list/extract on deeply nested entry paths, before `maxDepth` handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: Neither qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a drive-relative hardlink/symlink path traversal in extraction (`tar.x()`/`Unpack`) caused by stripping the absolute root after the `..` check; the session findings cover a separate archive-creation out-of-root read and a separate selective-filter stack-overflow denial of service.
evidence: The advisory patch `7bc755dd85e623c0279e08eb3784909e6d7e4b9f` moves `stripAbsolutePath()` ahead of `..` sanitization in `src/unpack.ts`, specifically for extracted link targets such as `C:../target.txt`.

`dotdot_create_arbitrary_file_read` affects `tar.create()`/`tar.update()`/`tar.replace()`, `Pack.add()`, and `WriteEntry` during archive creation and demonstrates out-of-root file reads, not extraction-time hardlink overwrite behavior.

`selective_filter_deep_path_stack_overflow` affects selective `list`/`extract` filtering through recursive `filesFilter().mapHas()` path walking and reproduces a `RangeError`, not the advisory's drive-relative link-target traversal.
